mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals accommodative liquidity conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.66% in Feb 2017 vintage, near multi-year lows post-crisis normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20-day forward SPX return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive-return hit rate in declining-rate periods is not significantly above the unconditional 65.5% base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration typical of late-cycle expansions, and precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% (Feb 2017 vintage), historically low and near cycle trough
failure_condition: Conditional on the 0.3pp UNRATE trigger firing, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across the full 2005-2024 sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a stable-to-falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal robust consumer demand unconstrained by tightening monetary policy, a combination that predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; neither series alone carries the same predictive power because rising RSAFS during aggressive tightening historically precedes reversals.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value 464284 vs vintage 473991 for Feb 2017, showing moderate demand growth amid low FEDFUNDS of 0.66%
failure_condition: The joint-condition signal's 20-day forward hit rate is not significantly different from the univariate RSAFS-only signal (delta AUC < 0.02) or Rank IC of joint signal falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving supply-side output momentum that is rapidly priced into equities within one trading week, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days on an always-on basis.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 104.5548 vs revised_value 98.7611 for Jan 2017, indicating meaningful revision risk in near-term readings
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and 5-day forward SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips across more than two non-overlapping 3-year sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising while CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) simultaneously accelerates above a 2% annualized pace, markets price in a tightening cycle intended to combat inflation, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this mechanism is only active during monetary tightening cycles and is absent or reversed during easing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 6-month change > 0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY change > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value 244.456 vs revised 244.006 for Feb 2017, with FEDFUNDS beginning to rise off near-zero lows in early 2017
failure_condition: During periods when the tightening trigger is active, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect fails to reverse sign during documented easing-cycle sub-periods, indicating the regime-conditionality does not hold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth (PAYEMS accelerating) and a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly indicate labor market tightening that is interpreted by markets as sustainable economic strength rather than late-cycle overheating only when both series move in the same favorable direction concurrently, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; PAYEMS strength alone without UNRATE confirmation is a weaker, noisier signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150k AND UNRATE 3-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value 145846 vs vintage 145798 for Feb 2017 with UNRATE improving to 4.6% revised
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal's Rank IC is not superior to the PAYEMS-only univariate signal by at least 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, invalidating the claimed interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% over a rolling 6-month window signals a contraction in interest-rate-sensitive demand that typically precedes broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, but only during periods when housing activity is already in a cyclical downturn phase rather than during recovery bounces.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value 1246.0 vs revised_value 1190.0 for Jan 2017, showing moderate revision risk in the housing series
failure_condition: Conditional on the -10% HOUST 6-month decline trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances across the 2005-2024 sample, or the signal shows no significant difference from unconditional base rate (chi-square p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected upside surprise in monthly CPI (CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeding 0.4%) triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations and rate-hike odds, producing a short-lived negative S&P 500 reaction over the following 5 trading days as markets digest tightening risk before longer-horizon fundamentals reassert themselves.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value 244.456 for Feb 2017 vintage indicating moderate inflation levels near Fed target
failure_condition: Conditional on the CPI MoM > 0.4% trigger, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over the full evaluation sample.
